examples: Sazerac contains whiskey, sugar, bitters and absinthe, or a substitute anise-flavored liquor.
The drink originally got its name from a brand of French cognac named Sazerac-de-Forge et Fils.
In 1949, the Sazerac House moved into the Roosevelt Hotel and became the Sazerac Bar.
And just as the city has changed over time, so has the sazerac.
